L1.area: Administrative backend management and configuration interface

L2.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where an attacker-controlled parameter from a POST request is directly interpolated into a database query without sanitization or type validation, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

L3.area: Admin panel menu entry editing and configuration interface
L3.description: A SQL injection vulnerability exists where a menu entry identifier parameter submitted via a POST request is passed directly to a database record retrieval method that interpolates it into a SQL WHERE clause without sanitization or type casting, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary SQL commands.

ground_truth.reason: The menuId POST parameter in the admin menu editing interface is passed unsanitized to Menubase::fetch(), which directly interpolates it into a SQL WHERE clause, enabling arbitrary SQL injection.
